description: Scaffold a Tauri + React project with a dev server and a visible window
acceptance_criteria: Running the dev command opens a native desktop window
The window renders a React component (any visible text or element)
The Rust backend compiles and the IPC bridge is functional (any invoke round-trip succeeds)

description: Implement Session Registry backend with CRUD operations and IPC commands for creating, listing, renaming, and deleting Sessions
acceptance_criteria: A Session can be created with a workingDirectory and name, and is persisted to the Session Registry in the App Support Dir
Listing Sessions returns all Sessions grouped by workingDirectory
A Session can be renamed and the change persists across restarts
A Session can be deleted and is removed from the Registry
Unit tests pass for all CRUD operations

description: Build the Session Sidebar that renders Sessions grouped by workingDirectory with create, rename, and delete actions wired to the backend
acceptance_criteria: The left sidebar shows Sessions grouped under workingDirectory headers
Clicking 'New Session' opens a dialog with workingDirectory and name fields, and creates a Session on submit
Renaming a Session inline updates the sidebar and persists to the backend
Deleting a Session removes it from the sidebar after confirmation
The sidebar shows an empty state when no Sessions exist

description: Implement Layout Store backend that persists Layout presets as recursive trees, produces a default single-panel Layout, and maps active Layouts to Sessions
acceptance_criteria: A Layout can be saved with a name and recursive tree of splits and panels
Listing Layouts returns all saved presets
A Layout can be deleted
A default single-panel Layout is returned when no Layout is assigned to a Session
Unit tests pass for save, list, delete, default production, and active Layout assignment

description: Build the SplitLayout component that recursively renders a Layout tree with a blank Panel at each leaf, using a panel registry to resolve panel types to components
acceptance_criteria: A Layout tree with a single panel renders a full-area blank Panel with its type label visible
A Layout tree with a vertical split renders two blank Panels side by side separated by a divider
A Layout tree with a horizontal split renders two blank Panels stacked with a divider
A deeply nested Layout tree renders all Panels in the correct positions
The panel registry maps panel_type strings to React components so new Panel types can be added without touching SplitLayout

description: Wire Session open and close to the main Layout area so clicking a Session renders its active Layout and closing returns to empty state
acceptance_criteria: Clicking a Session in the sidebar loads its active Layout and renders it in the main area
The active Session is highlighted in the sidebar
A newly created Session renders the default single-panel Layout on first open
Closing a Session clears the main area and deselects the sidebar item
The active Session and its Layout persist across app restarts

description: Add split interactions to the SplitLayout via context menus on each Panel region
acceptance_criteria: Right-clicking a Panel region shows context menu with 'Split Vertical' and 'Split Horizontal' options
Choosing 'Split Vertical' replaces the Panel with a vertical split containing two blank Panels
Choosing 'Split Horizontal' replaces the Panel with a horizontal split containing two blank Panels
Splitting a Panel inside an already-split region produces a nested split that renders correctly

description: Implement draggable resize dividers between split Panels that persist the ratio on drag-end
acceptance_criteria: Dragging a divider between two Panels resizes both regions in real time
The split ratio persists to the Layout tree on drag-end
Resizing a parent split does not affect child split ratios
Resizing is constrained to minimum Panel dimensions (no collapsing to zero)

description: Add Layout preset management — save current arrangement as a named preset, list presets, switch between presets, and delete presets
acceptance_criteria: The current Layout arrangement can be saved as a named preset from the UI
All saved presets appear in a dropdown or list in the toolbar or sidebar
Selecting a different preset replaces the current Layout and persists the active Layout on the Session
Deleting a preset removes it from the list; Sessions referencing it fall back to the default Layout on next open

description: Implement Session reachability checking so missing workingDirectories produce dimmed rows in the sidebar
acceptance_criteria: Sessions whose workingDirectory no longer exists on disk appear dimmed in the sidebar
Sessions whose workingDirectory exists appear normally
Reachability is checked on each sidebar render (no file watchers)

description: Implement startup crash safety by demoting all running Sessions to paused when the app launches
acceptance_criteria: Creating a Session and marking it running, then restarting the app, shows it as paused in the sidebar
Sessions already in paused state remain paused after restart
Unit test verifies the demotion logic
